PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline for all three components, then prioritize the one with the highest expected headroom gain per unit effort. First subtask should focus on identifying the dominant bottleneck and producing one clean improved candidate for the most promising component, while keeping the other two unchanged unless a clear low-cost improvement appears. If the first subtask yields a strong candidate, use the next turn to decide whether to allocate remaining effort to a second component or to refine the first. Keep the portfolio objective in mind: maximize total captured headroom, not just one component’s absolute quality.
